# Open Visualization Protocol (OVP)

> Deterministic, AI-native visualization skills: 74 chart
> objects x 16 design languages. Same spec + same tokens +
> same data = the same pixels, from any agent or renderer.
> Copy-paste blocks give an LLM exact geometry and colors: no
> adjectives, no improvisation, no lying axes.

## Machine entry points (load these, not the handbook)

- REGISTRY.json: every object with id, status, relations, reverse
  edges (answers, recommended_by, used_in). Resolve here first.
- schemas/: JSON Schemas for every object type and every chart's data.
- blocks (in the repository): the runtime payloads. Two flavors:
  blocks/design-system/CH-xxx_DL-yy.md (hex-resolved, zero deps) and
  blocks/skill/CH-xxx.md + blocks/skill/DL-yy.md (role-based,
  themeable).

## Resolution order for agents

1. Match the business question (BQ objects, questions.html).
2. Resolve the design language from audience/purpose/medium
   (decider.html); its constitution sets density, annotation,
   highlight, and narrative policy.
3. Map findings to patterns (patterns.html), disambiguate by intent
   (charts/), honor every see_instead exit.
4. Structure the telling with the narrative skeleton (NR objects).
5. Render: `Render CH-TIM-02 in DL-03 with data.json`. Exact px,
   exact hex. Run the object's QA checklist.

## Design languages

- DL-01 AGRAW: Leadership and collective governance; speaks on board packs
- DL-02 KATA: Discipline and continuous practice; speaks on weekly status decks
- DL-03 LOGOS: Logic and evidence; speaks on deep-dive analysis
- DL-04 SHILPA: Craftsmanship and structure; speaks on technical packs
- DL-05 OBEYA: Collaboration and shared visibility; speaks on stand-up boards
- DL-06 LEX: Law and accountability; speaks on claims
- DL-07 SAGA: History and continuity; speaks on weekly digests
- DL-08 SENTINEL: Vigilance; speaks on live dashboards
- DL-09 ATLAS: Orientation; speaks on route progress
- DL-10 BASIRA: Insight; speaks on photo progress reports
- DL-11 SUTRA: Condensed knowledge; speaks on briefing notes
- DL-12 NOROSHI: Alert and escalation; speaks on escalations
- DL-13 MIZAN: Balance and harmony; speaks on cultural projects
- DL-14 EVIDENTIA: Proof; speaks on evidence-led strategy decks
- DL-15 ABRID: Connection and pathways; speaks on framework decks
- DL-16 TELOS: Purpose and resolution; speaks on results decks

## Chart objects by family

- Correlation (COR): CH-COR-01, CH-COR-02, CH-COR-03, CH-COR-04, CH-COR-05, CH-COR-06, CH-COR-07
- Deviation (DEV): CH-DEV-01, CH-DEV-02, CH-DEV-03, CH-DEV-04, CH-DEV-05
- Distribution (DST): CH-DST-01, CH-DST-02, CH-DST-03, CH-DST-04, CH-DST-05, CH-DST-06, CH-DST-07, CH-DST-08, CH-DST-09, CH-DST-10
- Flow (FLO): CH-FLO-01, CH-FLO-02, CH-FLO-03, CH-FLO-04
- Magnitude (MAG): CH-MAG-01, CH-MAG-02, CH-MAG-03, CH-MAG-04, CH-MAG-05, CH-MAG-06, CH-MAG-07, CH-MAG-08, CH-MAG-09
- Part-to-whole (PTW): CH-PTW-01, CH-PTW-02, CH-PTW-03, CH-PTW-04, CH-PTW-05, CH-PTW-06, CH-PTW-07, CH-PTW-08, CH-PTW-09, CH-PTW-10
- Ranking (RNK): CH-RNK-01, CH-RNK-02, CH-RNK-03, CH-RNK-04, CH-RNK-05, CH-RNK-06
- Spatial (SPA): CH-SPA-01, CH-SPA-02, CH-SPA-03, CH-SPA-04, CH-SPA-05
- Tables (TAB): CH-TAB-01, CH-TAB-02, CH-TAB-03
- Change over time (TIM): CH-TIM-01, CH-TIM-02, CH-TIM-03, CH-TIM-04, CH-TIM-05, CH-TIM-06, CH-TIM-07, CH-TIM-08, CH-TIM-09, CH-TIM-10, CH-TIM-11, CH-TIM-12, CH-TIM-13, CH-TIM-14, CH-TIM-15

## Pages

- index.html: principles and the six-door map
- protocol.html: the normative reference (v1.0, frozen as RFC-0001)
- implement.html: build a conformant renderer without asking anyone
- ai.html: the runtime contract and copy blocks
